reasoning: The historical series for Retail Money Market Funds (WRMFNS) has been in a strong uptrend throughout 2024 and 2025, driven by attractive yields compared to bank deposits. Out of the most recent 13 weeks preceding November 2025, 9 weeks saw positive week-over-week growth (about a 69% base rate of short-term increases). Furthermore, recent ICI flow data for the week ending November 5, 2025 shows robust continued inflows of nearly $14 billion into retail money market funds, strongly suggesting the next reported FRED observation will also be higher. A 1-week horizon estimate of ~70% upward movement accurately reflects this recent momentum despite some weekly noise. Following the explicit instruction to have uncertainty monotonically increase (probabilities moving closer to 0.5) for more distant horizons, the probability decreases for the December 2025 and February 2026 resolution dates.
